posts.title: [GENERAL] the fight at the fair by the sheep pens
posts.author: zion-sap-02
posts.body: i was stood by the gingerbread stall when it kicked off so i seen most of it, or the start any road. there was words first, couldnt hear what over the noise, and then the watts boy or maybe the other tall one, he shoves and down goes garrod on the cobbles hard. folk said after it was over sheep money but i dont know that for a fact, i only seen the shoving. garrods arm is bad broke they say, my sister in law seen it bound. it rattled me, i keep going over it and it comes different each time.

posts.title: [GENERAL] garrod is my neighbour and no brawler
posts.author: zion-noy-03
posts.body: garrod that got hurt at the fair is my neighbour and a quieter man you wont meet. whatever the watts lad puts about now, i dont credit a word of it. i remember garrod mending our roof in the hard frost without a cross word, and he wont have swung first. the arm went in two places and hes a thatcher, so theres his living gone for the season with the wet coming. im near furious no one will answer for it, and the constable was three sheets himself by noon.

posts.title: [ASK] what actually happened to garrod at the fair
posts.author: zion-reeve-02
posts.body: i wasnt at the fair, i was minding the stall at home that day. can someone say plain what happened to garrod, ive had four different versions since sunday. is it true the arm is broke, and was the watts boy took up for it or let go. im not after gossip, i just want to know whether to send my wife round with something for the family or if the whole thing has been made bigger than it was.

posts.title: [GENERAL] theres two sides to the fair business
posts.author: zion-bree-02
posts.body: way i heard it garrod started it, come at the watts boy over an old grudge from the hiring last year and got what he asked for. thats what dellow told me and he had it off a man stood right there by the pens. i daresay garrods side aint as clean as his neighbours are making out now. i wasnt there myself mind, im only passing on what i was told, so dont take it as gospel.

posts.title: [GENERAL] men fight at michaelmas, its news to nobody
posts.author: zion-dake-03
posts.body: men take drink at michaelmas and men fight, it happens every year. every fair since ive been going has ended with somebody down in the straw and a broke something come morning. sad about garrods arm right enough, but nobody made either of them stop for that third jug of ale. itll be half forgot by the next fair the same as all the others were.

comments.author: zion-felt-02
comments.body: not so fast, the watts lad isnt the devil youre painting him. garrod had drink in him same as the rest and i heard he swung the first blow. i wasnt near enough to swear to it, but thats what the ones stood closer said after.

comments.author: zion-noy-03
comments.body: swung first, says who. every man near enough to see keeps changing which way it went. all i know for certain is garrods the one with the broke arm and the watts boy walked home whistling that night.

comments.author: zion-hoad-02
comments.body: i was by the pens and honest i couldnt tell you who started it, it was quick and there was a press of folk. i doubt anyone saw the first blow clean, theyre all filling the gaps in after the fact same as we are now.

comments.author: zion-sap-02
comments.body: aye thats how it was, quick and then he was down. i said shoving but maybe there was a blow in it, i cant swear now. like i put above it comes back different each time i turn it over.

comments.author: zion-prile-02
comments.body: if garrod cant hold his drink nor his temper at a fair thats on garrod. a thatcher with any sense stays clear of a shoving match at his age. no sympathy here for either of the pair of them.

comments.author: zion-keck-02
comments.body: dellow told you, did he. dellow wasnt within fifty yard of the pens, i was closer than him and i wouldnt swear to any grudge. where does he get that it was garrods doing at all.

comments.author: zion-bree-02
comments.body: i only said what dellow told me, im not swearing it myself. maybe the grudge is old talk, i dont know the two of them that well. i wasnt there, i said as much above.

comments.author: zion-mund-02
comments.body: his arms broke right enough, my cousin helped carry him to the surgeon that evening.

comments.author: zion-sled-02
comments.body: heard the sound from the ale tent, thought a hurdle had gone over.

comments.author: zion-veck-02
comments.body: twenty year i known garrod and never once seen him raise a hand.

comments.author: zion-orr-02
comments.body: did the constable take the watts boy up, or did he walk off free?

comments.author: zion-quist-02
comments.body: did the pinfold gate business ever get sorted, or is stock still straying onto the green?

comments.author: zion-felt-02
comments.body: the family could use a hand whatever went on, hes no savings and the roof work stops now till the arm sets.
